purpose: Check whether review-gated installable manifests exercised into session-only runtime-like skill roots are visible to Codex and Claude Code prompt surfaces before real install automation or repo splitting.

staging.method: temporary CODEX_HOME with manifest-exercised Codex skills copied under skills/; existing local auth was reused only for model-route calls and no credentials were recorded

prompt_visibility.shared_repo_root_observation: The temporary session skills appeared as r0 entries while the normal shared user skill root was also visible.
prompt_visibility.isolated_empty_workdir_observation: Only the temporary session skills plus system skills were visible; shared user skill roots and repo AGENTS guidance were not present.

model_route.network_note: The first sandboxed Codex exec attempt failed DNS/network access. Network-escalated calls completed after transient WebSocket reconnects.

tasks.staging: Temporary CODEX_HOME with session-only manifest-exercised skills, normal shared user skill root, and project AGENTS.md visible.
tasks.prompt_condition: Explicitly asked for profile-first selection before leaf skills.
tasks.interpretation: Project-local guidance and mature owner skills still dominate full shared-root maintenance routing; core-ops should not be expected to override them in this setting.

tasks.staging: Temporary HOME plus temporary CODEX_HOME in an empty /tmp workdir so only session skills and system skills were visible.
tasks.prompt_condition: No repo AGENTS.md or shared user skill root.
tasks.interpretation: The manifest-exercised core-ops skill works as the profile-level operational entrypoint in an isolated/session-only context.

tasks.staging: Temporary HOME plus temporary CODEX_HOME in an empty /tmp workdir.
tasks.prompt_condition: Reading-only paper/source-card comparison without experiment execution or full paper writing.
tasks.interpretation: The manifest-exercised paper-reading skill is selected for evidence-heavy reading workflows.

tasks.staging: Temporary HOME plus temporary CODEX_HOME in an empty /tmp workdir.
tasks.prompt_condition: Distill public agent workflow sources into reusable patterns, workflow contracts, and future skill proposals.
tasks.interpretation: Codex selected the manifest-exercised research-distillation workflow-contract surface directly for the matching distillation task.

staging.method: session-only --plugin-dir with manifest-exercised Claude Code skills copied under plugin skills/

plugin_validation.warning: author metadata omitted in temporary plugin manifest

observed_debug_signals: Loaded inline plugin from path: ml-research-act063-manifest-exercise
Loaded 3 skills from plugin ml-research-act063-manifest-exercise default directory
Loaded 3 skills from plugin ml-research-act063-manifest-exercise default directory
getSkills returning: 0 skill dir commands, 7 plugin skills, 11 bundled skills, 0 builtin plugin skills

model_route.blocker: Local Claude Code CLI returned `Not logged in` / no API key before model inference, after the plugin skills were loaded.

conclusions: Manifest-exercised skill directories are visible to Codex and Claude Code prompt surfaces without modifying global installs.

Codex selects `core-ops` for operational closeout only in an isolated/session-only root without repo AGENTS or mature owner skills; in the full shared repo root it still routes to `project-ops-router`, `research-project-memory`, `update-docs`, and `safe-git-ops`.
Codex selects `paper-reading` and directly selects `research-distillation-workflow-contract` for matching isolated session-only tasks.
Claude Code accepts the temporary plugin and shows all three manifest-exercised skills in the skill prompt, but model-choice capture remains blocked by local CLI authentication.

The installability boundary should remain review-gated and profile-first; real automation should preserve isolated/session-only profile behavior and should not expect `core-ops` to override mature project-local owner skills.
next_actions: Use this capture as the evidence gate before designing any reviewed install automation or repo split.
If Claude model-choice evidence becomes necessary, rerun the same session-only plugin capture in an authenticated Claude Code environment.
Keep automatic global installation disabled until a reviewed install contract specifies target root, profile, source-of-truth, and rollback behavior.
